title: What is a Locator? (The Simple Version)

items.description: Think of a locator as a 'Digital Address.' Just as a GPS needs a street address to find a house, an automation tool needs a locator to find a specific button or text box on a website.
Every element on a webpage (buttons, images, search bars) has hidden labels in the code. A locator tells the computer which label to look for so it knows exactly where to click.

title: How to Read a Locator Property

items.description: A locator property is a structured way to define how to find a web element in automation scripts. Each property should clearly specify the element's purpose, the method to locate it, and the exact value used for identification.
Locators are the foundation of Selenium automation. Here are the main types, their use cases, and practical examples.

subItems.title: ID Locator
subItems.description: Finds an element by its unique 'id'. This is the most reliable 'home address'.

subItems.title: Name Locator
subItems.description: Finds an element by its 'name' attribute, commonly used in input fields.

subItems.title: Class Name Locator
subItems.description: Finds elements by their CSS class. Useful for finding items that look a certain way.

subItems.title: Link Text Locator
subItems.description: Finds a link by the exact text shown to the user.

subItems.title: CSS Selector Locator
subItems.description: Uses style-based rules to find elements. Very fast and flexible.

subItems.title: XPath Locator
subItems.description: Finds elements by following a path through the website structure. Can find anything, but is sensitive to layout changes.

items.description: To a user, a button looks like a blue rectangle. To a computer, it looks like a line of code. Locators bridge that gap.

subItems.title: Step 1: Peek behind the curtain
subItems.description: We 'Inspect' the webpage to see the hidden labels like id='submit-btn'.

subItems.title: Step 2: Save the Address
subItems.description: We write it down: SubmitButton = id, submit-btn, Clicks the login button.

subItems.title: Step 3: Run the Command
subItems.description: The computer reads the 'SubmitButton' file and knows exactly where to click.
